<!--
2025-11-20

Documentation on imfcharts.

To generate this document run
quarto render imfcharts.qmd

Geneating PDF output will open a Carbon Black window
but ignoring it will render the document.

-->

---
title: "Documentation for imfcharts"
bibliography: references.bib
number-sections: true
page-layout: full
format:
  docx:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    highlight-style: github
    execute:
      echo: false

  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-float: true
    grid:
      body-width: 900px
      margin-width: 150px
      sidebar-width: 150px

#  pdf:
#    documentclass: report
#    papersize: letter

# jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: py-imports
import os
import sys

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib
# Need this, otherwise warning shows
# matplotlib.use('Qt5Agg')
# 'GTK3Agg', 'GTK3Cairo', 'GTK4Agg', 'GTK4Cairo', 'MacOSX', 'nbAgg', 'QtAgg', 'QtCairo', 'Qt5Agg', 'Qt5Cairo', 'TkAgg', 'TkCairo', 'WebAgg', 'WX', 


# Tables in quarto
from itables import show
from IPython.display import Markdown

from imfcharts import *
```

<!------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------>
# Introduction {#sec-intro}

The Fund wastes enormous time and effort...

This document covers the main usage and 
Most of the discussion is from[@Takagi; @Brodsky]
and based on experience with missions to Solomon Islands (2024) and Vanuatu (2025).


```{python}
#| label: fig1_chart1_gdp
#| fig-cap: "A plot with lines and bars."

infilename = '../data/turkey_page1/fig1_chart1_gdp.csv'
if not os.path.isfile(infilename):
    print('File ' + infilename + ' does not exist')
    sys.exit()
df = pd.read_csv(infilename, index_col=0, parse_dates=[0])
cols = df.columns
linecol = cols[0]
barcols = cols[1:]

# Create Chart object

dict_attrs = {linecol : {'linewidth' : 4,
                         'color' : '#004C97',
                         # 'linestyle' : '--',
                         'marker' : 'o',
                         'markersize' : 10,
                         'markerfacecolor' : 'white',
                         'markeredgewidth' : 4,
                         'markeredgecolor' : '#004C97'},
              'Consumption (private)' : {'color' : '#4B8CAD', 'barhatchcolor' : IMFBLUE, 'barhatch' : '\\\\'},
              'Consumption (public)' : {'color' : 'white', 'barhatch' : '\\\\\\', 'barhatchcolor' : IMFBLACK},
              'Net exports' : {'color' : IMFGREY},
              'Inventories/discrepancy' : {'color' : IMFRED},
              'Fixed investment' : {'color' : 'white',
                                    'barhatch' : '///',
                                    'barhatchcolor' : IMFGREEN,
                                    'barhatchwidth' : 10}
              }

chart1 = Chart(df, linecols=linecol, barcols=barcols,
               dict_attrs=dict_attrs,
               topxaxis='left',
               title='Contributions to Real GDP Growth',
               subtitle='(Percent, q/q)',
               xrange='2023Q1:', yrange=[-5, 9.5], ryrange=[-5, 9.5],
               ncol_legend=2)
chart1.add_hline(y=1.4, xrange='2023Q1:' + df.index[-1].strftime('%Y-%m'),
                 # xrange=[0.05, 0.95], coordinates='axis',
                 color=IMFBLACK, linewidth=2.5, linestyle='--', dashes=[10, 4])

text = '''Real GDP growth
long-run average
(1.4 q/q)'''

chart1.add_text(pd.Timestamp('2024Q3'), 6.5, text=text, fontweight='bold', color='#004C97')
chart1.add_arrow(head=[pd.Timestamp('2025Q1'), 1.5],
                 tail=[pd.Timestamp('2025Q1'), 5],
                 color='#004C97')
fig = chart1.fig
plt.show()
plt.close()
```

```{python}
#| label: fig1_chart2_labor
#| fig-cap: "A plot with lines"
# Read in data
infilename = '../data/turkey_page1/fig1_chart2_labor.csv'
if not os.path.isfile(infilename):
    print('File ' + infilename + ' does not exist')
    sys.exit()
df = pd.read_csv(infilename, index_col=0, parse_dates=[0])

# Create Chart object
chart2 = Chart(df, linecols=df.columns[0], rlinecols=df.columns[1],
               title='Labor Utilization',
               subtitle='(Percent)',
               xrange='2023-01:2025-08', # yrange=[8, 11], ryrange=[10, 25],
               yrange=[8, 11],
               )
               # debug=True)
plt.show()
plt.close()
```
